title: Highest temperature in Mexico City on June 11?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Benito Juárez International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 11 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Benito Juárez International Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/mx/mexico-city/MMMX.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models from sources including the National Meteorological Service of Mexico indicate a maximum temperature near 24–25°C for Mexico City on June 11, driven by typical early rainy-season conditions with daytime heating moderated by increasing cloud cover and scattered showers. High altitude of roughly 2,240 meters limits peak values compared with lower-elevation sites, while afternoon convection often caps temperatures below clear-sky potential. Minor differences among leading outcomes reflect uncertainty in exact timing and intensity of precipitation, which can reduce insolation and lower the daily high by 1–2°C depending on model runs. Historical June averages near 24–25°C provide baseline context, with current guidance showing no major deviations from seasonal norms.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent National Weather Service guidance and ensemble model runs indicate a heat wave peaking June 11, with forecast highs for New York City centered in the low-to-mid 90s amid southerly flow and building high pressure. This places the 92–95°F range as the most probable outcome according to trader consensus, consistent with departures 10–15°F above the early-June climatological average of roughly 79–82°F. Key variables differentiating the closely matched leading bins include the precise timing of peak heating, boundary-layer moisture affecting realized temperatures versus heat index, and any last-minute convective cooling. Updated model cycles and official observations from primary stations will determine final resolution within the narrow uncertainty band.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent ensemble forecasts from global and regional models point to a highest temperature in Seoul on June 12 most likely between 24°C and 25°C, consistent with typical early-June conditions before the full onset of the East Asian monsoon. Seoul’s climatological average daily high for the month hovers near 27–29°C, with day-to-day swings driven by shifting high-pressure ridges, frontal passages, and variable cloud cover that modulate surface heating. Current short-range guidance shows no strong anomalous warming or cooling signals, keeping probabilities tightly clustered around these two outcomes while assigning lower odds to 23°C or 26°C and beyond. Updated model runs and Korea Meteorological Administration briefings over the next 48 hours will provide the key data releases that traders monitor for shifts in implied probabilities.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Wellington Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/nz/wellington/NZWN.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Official MetService guidance projects a 14°C daytime maximum for Wellington on June 12 under mainly fine conditions with northerly winds shifting southerly by afternoon and a low shower risk. This aligns with early-winter climatology where June highs typically average 13–14°C, rarely exceeding 16°C absent strong warm advection. Trader consensus splits between 14°C and 15°C because subtle differences in cloud cover, wind persistence, or boundary-layer mixing could add 1°C of warming, while any earlier frontal passage would cap readings nearer 13°C. Short-range model runs and updated MetService briefings before the date will refine these thresholds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent weather model consensus from the Japan Meteorological Agency and international sources points to a Tokyo maximum near 25–26°C on June 12 amid the East Asian monsoon’s influence and typical early-summer variability. Cloud cover, scattered showers, and urban heat-island effects introduce uncertainty in afternoon peak readings, keeping trader probabilities tightly clustered across 24–27°C outcomes. Historical June data show average highs around 26°C with a standard deviation of roughly 2–3°C, underscoring how small shifts in steering patterns or precipitation timing can alter the daily high by a degree or two. Updated short-range forecasts and real-time observations over the next 48 hours will refine these projections ahead of market resolution.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 12 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shanghai/ZSPD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ensemble forecasts from global numerical weather prediction models currently cluster Shanghai’s June 12 daytime maximum in the low-30s Celsius range, supporting the market’s leading 31°C outcome. Typical early-summer conditions feature subtropical moisture and variable convective cloud cover that can suppress or enhance peak readings by 1–2°C, while subtle steering wind shifts influence afternoon heating. Historical June averages near 29°C provide a baseline, yet recent model runs indicate modestly warmer conditions. Fresh satellite, surface, and upper-air observations over the next 48 hours will refine the China Meteorological Administration guidance and international ensembles, narrowing the broad probability spread across 29–33°C before the official daily high is recorded.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Singapore's June 12 daily maximum centers on 31–32 °C because official NEA forecasts and ensemble models indicate typical equatorial June conditions with afternoon thundery showers that often cap surface heating near these values. Persistent high humidity and cloud cover from the southwest monsoon reduce insolation, while localized convective activity can either suppress or briefly elevate readings depending on timing and location relative to Changi station. El Niño development adds slight seasonal warmth but exerts minimal influence on this short-range outcome. New 24–48 hour model runs and NEA daily updates will refine the exact threshold before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Persistent Meiyu frontal activity and southwesterly flow continue to deliver widespread cloud cover, showers, and thunderstorms across northern Taiwan, sharply limiting solar insolation and capping afternoon maxima well below the June climatological average of 30–33 °C. Official Central Weather Administration guidance and ensemble model consensus point to a June 12 high in the mid- to upper-20s °C, with the precise peak hinging on the timing and coverage of precipitation. This uncertainty explains the tight clustering of trader-implied probabilities around 26–28 °C, as even modest shifts in rainfall timing or intensity can alter the daily maximum by 1–2 °C. Updated CWA forecasts and high-resolution model runs expected over the next 48 hours will provide the clearest signals ahead of market resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus clusters around 31–33°C for Guangzhou’s June 12 high, consistent with the city’s subtropical monsoon climate where June averages reach 31°C amid 80–85% humidity and frequent afternoon convection. Short-range model runs show modest spread driven by variable cloud cover, thunderstorm timing, and steering from the western Pacific subtropical high, which can either enhance or suppress daytime heating by 1–2°C. Recent observational trends indicate typical early-summer instability without strong anomalous ridging or tropical influences that would push extremes higher, keeping probabilities for 34°C or above under 10% while lower outcomes reflect potential for enhanced rainfall cooling. Updated regional forecasts expected in the next 24–48 hours will likely refine these ranges.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest numerical weather prediction models show Qingdao under a building ridge of high pressure that favors daytime heating and limited cloud cover, producing forecast highs clustered tightly around 30–32°C for June 12. Ensemble guidance from global and regional models exhibits modest spread in boundary-layer mixing and afternoon sea-breeze timing, keeping the 31 °C and 32 °C+ bins nearly tied at 28 % each while the 30 °C outcome trails at 22.5 %. Sea-surface temperatures in the Yellow Sea near 20 °C provide a moderating influence that historically caps extremes this early in June, yet any delay in onshore flow or slightly stronger insolation could push readings one degree higher. Refined short-range runs and local observational updates over the next 48 hours will narrow the remaining uncertainty before market resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent PAGASA forecasts for Metro Manila on June 12 project daytime highs of 32–33°C under the influence of the southwest monsoon, which brings increased cloud cover, scattered thunderstorms, and moderating moisture that limits extreme heating. These conditions align with climatological June averages near 32°C while introducing variability from convective activity that could briefly push peaks to 34°C or hold them at 33°C depending on timing and intensity of rain. Official outlooks emphasize partly cloudy to cloudy skies with isolated showers, creating the tight clustering of market-implied odds around 33–34°C as traders weigh model consensus against the potential for localized warming or enhanced rainfall suppression of temperatures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest numerical weather prediction models, including ECMWF and AEMET guidance, show Madrid under stable high pressure with clear skies and light northeasterly flow, supporting a daytime maximum near 34°C on June 12. This positions the 34°C outcome at 49.5% market-implied probability, ahead of 35°C at 26.5% and 33°C at 19.0%, reflecting slight model spread on peak heating. June climatology places average highs around 28–30°C, but the current early-summer ridge has already produced readings near 32°C in recent days, tilting trader consensus toward the upper end of the typical range while acknowledging uncertainty from final model runs expected overnight.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by NOAA at the Vnukovo International Airport in degrees Celsius on 12 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from NOAA, specifically the highest reading under the "Temp" column for all times on this day, available here: https://www.weather.gov/wrh/timeseries?site=UUWW

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the "Switch to Metric Units" button until the relevant table displays °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest numerical weather models indicate Moscow's June 12 daytime high will likely peak near 30°C, with trader positioning reflecting the spread across ensemble runs from major global forecasting centers. Variability stems primarily from uncertain afternoon cloud cover, timing of any weak frontal passage, and local wind patterns that modulate surface heating. Historical June maxima in Moscow average around 22–24°C, so current guidance points to above-normal warmth driven by a persistent ridge, though small shifts in these variables could easily push the observed high into the 28–32°C range. The broad distribution across outcomes underscores the inherent uncertainty in short-range temperature forecasts just 48 hours out, with official station data from Roshydromet providing the definitive resolution metric.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecast models from sources including the Met Office and BBC Weather indicate a daytime high near 19°C for Amsterdam on June 12, driven by moderate westerly winds and Atlantic maritime air masses typical of early June in the Netherlands. eventMetadata.context_description: Jeddah’s coastal Red Sea location and persistent subtropical high pressure support peak temperatures in the mid-to-upper 30s Celsius during early June, consistent with climatological averages near 36–38 °C. Short-range forecasts from agencies such as AccuWeather show daily highs ranging 34–40 °C through mid-month under clear skies and light winds, with minimal cloud cover or moisture to suppress daytime heating. Recent observations of 35 °C maxima and dust-storm conditions reinforce the baseline, keeping the 37 °C-or-higher outcome at 59 % market-implied probability while the 36 °C band holds secondary weight. Model consensus indicates stable steering patterns through June 12, though any unexpected increase in humidity or onshore flow could cap peaks near 36 °C.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders see the highest temperature in Cape Town on June 12 most likely in the 16–18°C range because current forecast models from agencies like the South African Weather Service converge on mild winter conditions typical for the region, with average June daily highs near 16–17°C based on long-term observations. Model spreads reflect inherent uncertainty in short-term steering patterns and local orographic effects around Table Mountain, keeping probabilities distributed across several degrees rather than concentrated on a single outcome. eventMetadata.context_description: Recent observations and short-range forecast models from agencies like the Pakistan Meteorological Department indicate peak daytime temperatures in Karachi near 36–38°C through mid-June, driven by strong solar insolation ahead of the monsoon and typical pre-monsoon atmospheric stability. Coastal humidity and sea breezes moderate extremes, aligning with historical June averages of 34–36°C while current runs show limited potential for spikes above 39°C absent major heat advection. This positions 37°C as the market leader, reflecting trader consensus on stable conditions through the June 12 resolution window, with updates from daily model runs likely to refine probabilities as land-sea interactions evolve.
